brands.name: FR Skates
brands.widthDisclaimer: FR Skates feature a wide shell profile. If you are between sizes, stay true to size or go half a size down for more control.

brands.name: SEBA
brands.widthDisclaimer: SEBA boots run narrow. If you have a wide foot, go half a size up from your mm recommendation. If you are average or narrow width, size true.

brands.name: USD Skates
brands.widthDisclaimer: USD aggressive skates fit an average-width foot. Size true to your mm measurement. Check the specific model page for shell differences between the Aeon and Carbon ranges.

brands.name: Rollerblade
brands.widthDisclaimer: Rollerblade uses a standard medium-width last across most models. Size true to your mm recommendation. Wider models (e.g. Zetrablade) may give more room if you are borderline wide.

brands.name: Powerslide
brands.widthDisclaimer: Powerslide offers models in both narrow (Next, Trinity) and wide (Megacruiser) last profiles. Size true to your mm recommendation and verify the specific model width on the product page.

brands.name: CHAYA
brands.widthDisclaimer: CHAYA roller skates fit an average foot width. If you are between sizes, go half a size up for more comfort or half a size down for tighter control.

brands.name: Moxi Skates
brands.widthDisclaimer: Moxi skates are known for a roomy toe box and suit average to wide feet well. Narrow-footed skaters may want to go half a size down.

brands.name: Rio Roller
brands.widthDisclaimer: Rio Roller skates suit average-width feet. Size true to your mm measurement. The Classic models tend to run slightly narrow compared to the Style range.

brands.name: Rossignol
brands.widthDisclaimer: Rossignol alpine boots use a standard medium-width last. If you have a wide foot, consider sizing half a Mondopoint up or trying the Lange LX range.

brands.name: Lange
brands.widthDisclaimer: Lange boots traditionally run narrow. If you have an average or wide foot, go half a Mondopoint size up from your foot measurement.

brands.name: Fischer
brands.widthDisclaimer: Fischer boots suit average to medium-wide feet. Use your foot measurement directly; if you are between Mondopoint sizes, go half a size up.
